The classification of Richterago stenophylla reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plant species in the Compositae family.

intro: Richterago stenophylla, commonly known as Richtera, is a perennial plant species belonging to the saxifrage family (Saxifragaceae), native to mountainous regions of Central Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its potential health benefits in reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Richterago stenophylla include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Richtera is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in the production of capsules and tablets for the treatment of various health conditions.
